gate_mode_rationale: The release changes mandatory task classification and acceptance policy for all downstream projects.

scope_rationale.web: The change affects a dependency-free JSON validator and documentation, not a web UI.
scope_rationale.public_deploy: No public deployment is part of this release.
scope_rationale.integration: The release changes task-contract policy without invoking an external integration.
scope_rationale.stateful: No authoritative product data or authorization ledger is changed.

outcome.beneficiary: MVP owners and agents using MVP OS to avoid wasteful implementation.
outcome.observable_result: MVP OS accepts a reduced contract only for bounded local work and requires the full contract for features, integrations, external actions, product experiments, and conditional scopes.
acceptance_criteria: Short mode passes only for allowed local work types with every conditional scope disabled.
Full mode requires beneficiary, result owner, non-goals, minimum proof, complete outcome chain, cost and scale limits, and structured reuse evaluation.
Independent review records explicit outcome, component-substitution, reuse, evidence, end-to-end, and cost-scale checks.
Canonical and project-template skill trees remain byte-identical.
non_goals: Do not change Project Control schema v2.
Do not alter web, integration, state, or Hermes acceptance semantics.
Do not auto-merge downstream project updates.
outcome.minimum_proof: Positive and negative validator tests, full repository verification, and two independent reviews pass against one source commit.
outcome.realistic_data: Positive and negative JSON fixtures exercise short, full, legacy, feature, product-experiment, integration, and conditional-scope contracts.
outcome.external_result: A published MVP OS release exposes the validator and templates through the existing reviewed sync path.
outcome.cost_scale_contract: No runtime dependency is added; validation remains one local Python process and contract size grows only with task complexity.
outcome.time_budget: Stop if a backward-compatible implementation cannot be verified in one bounded release slice.
outcome.stop_condition: Stop before publication on legacy-contract regression, canonical/template drift, unresolved review findings, or failed Project Control verification.
forbidden_simplifications: Do not weaken full mode to make short mode easier.
Do not classify product features or external actions as short local work.
outcome.rollback: Keep v2.1.0 published and do not advance VERSION, tag, or downstream desired state if verification fails.

chain.external_signal: The accepted incident correction and IMPLEMENT authorization define the requested policy change.
chain.interpretation: The current v2.1.0 gate is compared against the accepted proportionality requirements.
chain.decision_policy: Short mode is limited to bounded local work; all higher-risk work remains full mode.
chain.action: Validator, templates, docs, tests, decisions, and release metadata are updated.
chain.external_result: A verified tagged release becomes available to active projects through reviewed sync.
chain.product_display: CLI gate output and durable release/context records expose the result.
chain.independent_verification: Automated tests, Project Control verification, and separate clean-context and implementation reviews pass.

discovery.rationale: Extend the existing dependency-free gate_check.py validator and synchronized templates.

evaluation.maintenance: Existing validator is actively maintained in MVP OS.
evaluation.security: No external execution or data transfer added.
evaluation.lock_in: No new lock-in; contract remains plain JSON and Python standard library.
evaluation.integration_cost: Small extension to existing validation and tests.

discovery.remaining_gap: v2.1.0 has no proportional mode, work classification, richer outcome ownership, or explicit reviewer checks.

discovery.rationale: The task is local policy validation and has no external platform API.

evaluation.maintenance: Not applicable to local policy validation.
evaluation.integration_cost: Any external API would add unjustified cost.

discovery.rationale: No platform connector participates in offline task-contract validation.

evaluation.integration_cost: Connector setup would be pure overhead.

discovery.rationale: An external policy engine would add dependency and integration cost without matching MVP OS contracts.

evaluation.maintenance: Would create a second maintenance boundary.
evaluation.lock_in: Would couple contracts to another policy model.
evaluation.integration_cost: Higher than extending the existing validator.

discovery.rationale: A hosted service would reduce offline reliability and add unnecessary cost and data exposure.

evaluation.maintenance: Introduces provider operations and availability risk.
evaluation.license: Introduces service terms and commercial review.
evaluation.security: Would transmit project contract data externally.

reuse.custom_code_reason: A small extension to the existing validator is the missing layer and preserves dependency-free offline enforcement.

preflight_review.summary: Full-mode classification, outcome, reuse decision, evidence plan, end-to-end chain, and cost/scale constraints are coherent and complete; no blocking findings.
